followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: First cycle — no prior review to compare.

main_claims.claim: Any D-dimensional static black hole with g_tt*g_rr = -1 can be reconstructed as a vacuum solution to a D-dimensional generally covariant quasi-topological gravitational theory
main_claims.notes: The reconstruction procedure is the central result, but it is not proved in this paper — it is attributed entirely to the companion preprint Borissova (2026). This paper only summarises the procedure and cites the key steps back to that reference. Without access to the full proof there, the claim cannot be independently verified here.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract and 'Extended quasi-topological gravities' section, citing Borissova (2026) arXiv:2603.06786

main_claims.claim: The generating function G in the integrated equation of motion (eq. 24) determines the thermodynamic mass M in a generalised first law with entropy given by the Wald entropy
main_claims.notes: The logical chain (integrated EOM → mass identification → Hawking temperature from surface gravity → Wald entropy via linear dependence of 2D Lagrangian on Ricci scalar → first law) is described coherently in prose. However, because all equations are unrendered in the HTML bundle, the explicit formulas cannot be checked. The argument that the 2D Lagrangian depends only linearly on R̄ (making the variational derivative a partial derivative) is plausible for the Horndeski sector relevant here but is stated without displayed derivation.
main_claims.paper_location: Equations (24), (35), (46)–(48); 'Thermodynamics of black holes' and 'Wald entropy' sections

main_claims.claim: The integrability condition (eq. 9) is necessary and sufficient for the existence of static solutions and for the equation of motion to integrate to an algebraic equation
main_claims.notes: The sufficiency direction (integrability condition implies integrability of EOM) is a non-trivial claim. The paper asserts it and defers to companion preprints. Since eq. (9) is not rendered, the condition itself cannot be examined.
main_claims.paper_location: 'Extended quasi-topological gravities' section, eq. (9); citing Borissova (2026) and Borissova & Carballo-Rubio (2026b)

main_claims.claim: A Birkhoff theorem holds for QTGs: vacuum solutions of the form (20) are necessarily static and satisfy g_tt*g_rr = -1
main_claims.notes: Entirely attributed to the companion paper; the argument given here is only the statement. Proving a Birkhoff theorem for a broad non-polynomial class of higher-curvature theories is nontrivial and the present paper offers no independent verification.

main_claims.claim: The Bardeen AdS regular black hole example satisfies the first law with the thermodynamic quantities computed in eqs. (70)–(72)
main_claims.notes: The step-by-step calculation described in the prose is standard in structure (read off mass from asymptotics, invert for G, compute T from surface gravity, S from Wald formula). The prose says 'the first law (48) can be verified', but since eqs. (67)–(72) are unrendered, one cannot check the algebra or confirm consistency with known results for this black hole.

main_claims.claim: The entropy formula (46) reduces to the Bekenstein-Hawking area law for GR, and more generally reproduces known entropy results for polynomial curvature QTGs
main_claims.notes: The footnote 8 asserts consistency with results in Bueno et al. (2025d), Hennigar et al. (2025), and Aguayo et al. (2025) for polynomial curvature QTGs. This is a sanity check that the formula passes, but equation (46) itself is unrendered so the check cannot be made explicit here.
main_claims.paper_location: Eq. (46), footnote 8; 'Example' section: limit statement for Schwarzschild-AdS

missing_assumptions.assumption: The event horizon r_h is a simple (non-degenerate) zero of f(r), so that the surface gravity κ is non-zero and the Hawking temperature formula (eq. 36) applies
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The paper claims applicability to both singular and regular black holes. Regular black holes can have extremal or near-extremal horizons where f'(r_h) → 0 and the Hawking temperature T → 0. In those cases the Euclidean periodicity argument for temperature breaks down or requires separate treatment. The paper does not explicitly restrict to non-degenerate horizons.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The integrability condition (eq. 9) can always be satisfied by an appropriate choice of generating function G for any given static black hole metric with g_tt*g_rr = -1
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Opening of 'Extended quasi-topological gravities' section and the reconstruction discussion
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The reconstruction procedure (any such black hole becomes a QTG vacuum solution) is presented as universally applicable. If the integrability condition imposes constraints that not all smooth metrics satisfy, the scope of the framework is narrower than claimed.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The D-dimensional generally covariant theory (eq. 2) reconstructed from the 2D Horndeski theory via the uplift procedure is well-defined and unique (up to gauge/field-redefinition equivalences) for a given black hole metric
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If the uplift is not unique, different D-dimensional theories could yield different Wald entropies for the same 2D effective theory, undermining the claim of a theory-independent thermodynamic framework.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The 2D boundary terms do not contribute to the Wald entropy and can be neglected in the reduction
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Final remarks, noting that boundary terms for the D-dimensional theory are 'generally unknown'
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The paper acknowledges this as an open question when comparing with Euclidean path integral methods. If boundary contributions shift the entropy relative to the bulk Wald formula, the first law as derived would be incomplete.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Verify that the Wald entropy formula (eq. 46) and mass formula (eq. 35) explicitly reproduce the known thermodynamic quantities (Bekenstein-Hawking entropy, ADM mass, correct temperature) for (i) Schwarzschild-AdS in arbitrary D, (ii) Gauss-Bonnet black holes, and (iii) the Bardeen AdS black hole in 4D
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: All numbered equations in the bundle are unrendered. A human reviewer can access the PDF directly and verify the displayed formulas against the literature.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Check whether the Wald entropy reduction (eqs. 40–44) correctly handles the G_5 sector of the 2D Horndeski theory — specifically whether the variational derivative of the D-dimensional Lagrangian with respect to R_μνρσ is fully captured by the partial derivative of the reduced 2D Lagrangian with respect to R̄
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires working out the full Horndeski decomposition and comparing the D-dimensional entropy tensor contraction with the 2D formula explicitly, which cannot be done without the displayed equations.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Assess whether the companion papers (especially Borissova 2026 arXiv:2603.06786 and Carballo-Rubio 2026 arXiv:2507.15920) contain complete proofs of the reconstruction theorem and Birkhoff theorem that this letter relies on
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The present reviewer cannot access and evaluate multiple companion preprints in parallel within this review. The validity of the present letter's central claims is conditional on those papers being correct.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Verify the principle-of-symmetric-criticality step for compact hyperbolic horizon topology: does the argument in Palais (1979) / Fels & Torre (2002) apply when the local symmetry group of the fibre is SO(D-2,1) (non-compact) and only the quotient by a discrete lattice is compact?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This is a group-theoretic question about whether the compact symmetry group hypothesis of those theorems applies to the cocompact lattice quotient case. It requires access to the original references and careful comparison.

novelty_assessment: The novelty is genuine but incremental. The key advance is extending the quasi-topological gravity thermodynamics framework — previously developed for polynomial and some non-polynomial curvature QTGs by Bueno, Cano, Hennigar and co-workers — to a broader class that includes phenomenological (quantum-gravity inspired) black hole models. The connecting device is the 2D dilaton reduction: by treating any static g_tt*g_rr=-1 black hole as a vacuum of an extended QTG via the generating function construction, one automatically inherits Wald's formalism without knowing the explicit D-dimensional Lagrangian. The generalised first law with P-V chemistry and multi-coupling Smarr formula is a natural extension of known results. The synthesis is clean and the Bardeen AdS example is a useful proof-of-concept. The primary novelty relative to the companion papers (Borissova 2026; Borissova & Carballo-Rubio 2026a,b) is the thermodynamic application of the reconstruction framework, rather than the reconstruction itself.
score_rationale: Technical soundness (6): The logical architecture is coherent and the framework is grounded in established results (Wald entropy, Horndeski reduction, integrability). However, the key reconstruction existence proof is entirely deferred to companion preprints; the G_5 sector of the Wald entropy reduction deserves more justification; and all equations are unrendered in the supplied bundle, preventing algebraic verification. Novelty (6): A genuine and useful synthesis that extends thermodynamic access to phenomenological black holes, but the hard work (reconstruction theorem, integrability) was done in companion papers. Significance (7): Provides a practically usable unified formula for entropy and mass that applies to models widely used in quantum gravity phenomenology, which is timely given the growth of the regular black hole literature. Clarity (5): The prose is well-structured and the conceptual flow is clear, but the HTML rendering failure means the paper is effectively equation-free in this bundle. The heavy reliance on concurrent preprints for foundational claims also reduces stand-alone readability. Reviewer confidence (4): Substantially reduced by the equation rendering failure — the review is effectively based on the prose skeleton of a mathematical paper.
summary: This 7-page letter by Borissova establishes a unified framework for the thermodynamics of D-dimensional static black holes satisfying g_tt * g_rr = -1 in Schwarzschild gauge. The core strategy is two-fold: (i) interpret any such black hole as a solution to an integrable 2-dimensional effective dilaton theory (specifically a 2D Horndeski theory satisfying an integrability condition), and (ii) lift this 2D theory to a D-dimensional quasi-topological gravity (QTG) in a generalised sense. The generating function G appearing in the integrated equation of motion is then identified as the thermodynamic mass. The Wald entropy is computed by reducing the entropy tensor calculation to the 2D Lagrangian, which depends linearly on the 2D Ricci scalar. A generalised first law including pressure-volume terms and potentials conjugate to higher-curvature couplings is derived, together with a Smarr-type formula via Euler's theorem for homogeneous mass functions. The framework is illustrated on the asymptotically AdS Bardeen regular black hole. A major limitation of this review is that the HTML source renders all numbered equations (1)–(72) as empty table cells, so the actual mathematical content of every key formula is unverifiable from the supplied bundle.

technical_objections.evidence: The HTML source represents each equation as an empty table cell. The reviewer has access only to prose descriptions and equation labels. This is a systematic failure of the source material supplied, not a flaw in the paper itself, but it fundamentally limits auditability.
technical_objections.objection: All numbered equations (1)–(72) are unrendered in the HTML bundle, making independent algebraic verification of every key result impossible from the supplied source

technical_objections.evidence: The text states e.g. 'Any such spacetime … can be constructed as a vacuum solution to a quasi-topological gravitational theory [Borissova (2026)]' and 'the generating function can be reconstructed which make the configuration (30)–(31) a solution… [Boyanov & Carballo-Rubio (2025); Carballo-Rubio (2026)]'. The self-contained contribution of the present paper is limited to the thermodynamic application, while the existence and properties of the underlying theory are borrowed.
technical_objections.objection: The paper's primary results rest on a chain of companion preprints (Borissova 2026 arXiv:2603.06786; Borissova & Carballo-Rubio 2026a arXiv:2601.17115; 2026b arXiv:2602.16773; Carballo-Rubio 2026 arXiv:2507.15920; Boyanov & Carballo-Rubio 2025 arXiv:2506.14875). The reconstruction theorem, integrability condition, Birkhoff theorem, and explicit reconstruction formulas (eqs. 32–34) are all imported from these references without proof in the present letter.
technical_objections.paper_location: Throughout; especially the 'Extended quasi-topological gravities' section and footnotes 5, 11

technical_objections.evidence: The paper says 'The former depends only linearly on [2D Ricci scalar] via the 2-dimensional Ricci scalar in (8), and in particular does not depend on covariant derivatives of the curvature.' The G_5 term in Horndeski theory generates, via the scalar-Gauss-Bonnet-type coupling, contributions to the Wald entropy that involve the Gauss-Bonnet tensor and are not transparently linear in R̄. The prose assertion of linearity deserves a displayed derivation.
technical_objections.objection: The Wald entropy reduction to the 2D Lagrangian (eq. 44) relies on the claim that the reduced Lagrangian density depends on the D-dimensional Riemann tensor only through the 2D Ricci scalar (eq. 42), so the entropy tensor variation reduces to a partial derivative. For the most general Horndeski theory including G_5(φ,X) terms, the D-dimensional Lagrangian of the extended QTG may involve higher curvature invariants whose variation with respect to R_μνρσ is not captured by the 2D reduction alone.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper writes 'according to the principle of symmetric criticality which holds for compact symmetry groups'. For compact hyperbolic topology the local symmetry group used in the warped-product reduction is SO(D-2,1), which is not compact, though the quotient space itself may be compact. The subtlety is not addressed.
technical_objections.objection: The principle of symmetric criticality is invoked to equate field equations of the full D-dimensional theory with those from the reduced 2D theory. The paper acknowledges this holds 'for compact symmetry groups'. For compact hyperbolic horizon topology, the symmetry group is a discrete cocompact lattice Γ acting on hyperbolic space; it is not the continuous isometry group SO(D-2,1), which is non-compact. Whether the principle of symmetric criticality applies to the quotient space H^{D-2}/Γ is not discussed.
technical_objections.paper_location: Prose following eq. (12), citing Palais (1979), Fels & Torre (2002), Deser & Tekin (2003)

technical_objections.evidence: The paper says 'the mass can be written as [eq. 35]' following from the integrated EOM, but the derivation of which function of c equals M in the general case is not shown explicitly. For phenomenological models this requires knowing the asymptotics in detail.
technical_objections.objection: The identification of the integration constant c with the thermodynamic mass M (eq. 35) is presented as immediate from the integrated EOM (eq. 24), but no boundary/normalization condition is stated in the letter to pin down this identification uniquely for non-polynomial generating functions. For the GR case the identification with ADM mass is shown (eqs. 26–29), but for general G the justification is implicit.
technical_objections.paper_location: Eq. (35); prose 'We will see that this identification provides the thermodynamic mass'
